verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP5B) is canonically the beta subunit of the F1 catalytic core on the inner mitochondrial membrane. However, extensive peer-reviewed evidence documents its ectopic translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane — termed 'ecto-ATP synthase' (eATP synthase) — in tumor cells (breast, prostate, AML, liver, lung carcinoma) and some normal cell types (endothelial cells, hepatocytes). The intact complex is transported from mitochondria to the cell surface via microtubule-dependent membrane fusion. Aptamers and monoclonal antibodies targeting ecto-ATP5B bind intact cells and exert functional/cytotoxic effects, confirming outer-leaflet accessibility. This surface pool is restricted to specific oncogenic/cell-state contexts and certain normal cell types, not a baseline feature of most somatic cells.
